celebration: 🎉 WHY EARNS ITS LETTERS. Since Week 32 'why' has been a heart word, taken on faith. Today the class learns y-as-a-vowel and spells it at last: wh-y. It leaves the heart-word wall and joins the readable words — a promise kept, thirteen weeks later.

songs.title: Why-My-Fly!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(y… y… y…)
y can be a vowel!

[Hook]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
At the end, y says I!
Why-my-fly! Why-my-fly!
Why? My! Fly! (up high!)

[Verse 2]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
Cry! Try! Sky! Shy!
One little y — say I!
(fly! fly! fly!)

[Verse 3]
Y! Y! Y-y-y!
At the end of a long word — say EE!
Happy! Puppy! Silly! Sunny!
(ee! ee! ee!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not y!
(y… y… y…)

[Outro]
little word — y says I… long word — y says ee…
Why? (up high!)
(y… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: y-as-vowel does TWO jobs: at the end of a SHORT word it says long-I /aɪ/ (my, fly, why, cry) — hook 1+2; at the end of a LONG word it says long-E /iː/ (happy, puppy, silly) — verse 3. Keep the two jobs cleanly split. 🎉 'Why' is spelled aloud this week for the first time (wh-y) — let the choir cheer it. Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: Why, Fly, Why?
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Why, fly, why?
Why, fly, why?
Where is the fly? (where? where?)
Up in the sky!

[Verse 2]
The fly is shy.
The fly can not fly!
Try, fly, try! (try! try!)
Why, fly, why?

[Verse 3]
Bug can help the fly.
Bee can help the fly.
Fly, fly, fly! (up high!)
The fly is happy!

[Outro]
The fly… the sky… (shhh…)
What is up so high?!
(POTATO!)
Why, fly, why? (we can spell it now!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Why, fly, why?' — a shy fly who can't fly, cheered on by Bug and Bee. The word 'why' is the star: sung and now spellable (wh-y). The outro names the milestone ('we can spell it now!'). Bounce the /aɪ/ words (why, fly, sky, try, shy) high. Whisper before the potato, which is up in the sky somehow.

spreads.artNote: A little cartoon housefly with big friendly eyes in the spotlight, wings folded, looking a bit glum.
The fly slumped on a leaf, wings drooping. 'Why' — the heart word since Week 32 — printed now, decodable at last: wh-y.
The fly hiding behind a blade of grass, peeking out shyly. 'Shy' — sh + y.
The fly flapping hard but staying stuck on the ground, a little dust cloud, comic effort.
Bug — the round red-shelled beetle — trundling up with an encouraging look, a tiny grass ramp under one arm.

spreads.text: Bee can help. Try, fly, try!
spreads.artNote: Bee — the plump gold-and-black honeybee — hovering, cheering the fly on. 'Try' — tr + y.
Lift-off! The fly airborne at last, wings a blur, Bug and Bee cheering below.
The fly looping joyfully in the air. 'Happy' — the OTHER y, saying ee. The two jobs of y on one spread.
High in the night sky, tangled in a tiny paper kite, floats a single potato with the fly buzzing happy circles round it. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

extras: 🎉 THE PROMISE KEPT: back in Week 32, when what/where/why joined the heart wall, the class was told 'why will earn its letters one day.' Today is that day. Take why down off the heart-word wall with ceremony and hang it among the readable words — the class SPELLS it (wh-y) for the first time. Two heart words in the whole program earn their letters this way: 'on' (Week 11) and 'why' (today).

THE TWO JOBS OF y: at the end of a SHORT word, y says long-I (my, fly, why, cry, sky). At the end of a LONG word, y says long-E (happy, puppy, silly, candy). One letter, a job that depends on the length of the word — sort a stack and let them discover the pattern.
Pattern Tree: the class reads my/fly/why/happy aloud and hangs the y-vowel leaf.
Moveable alphabet: build m-y, fl-y, wh-y, cr-y, then the long words happ-y, pupp-y — feeling the y flip from I to ee.

teacherFocus: y is the letter that becomes a vowel at the end of a word, and it does two jobs by word-length: short word → long-I (my, fly, why, cry); long word → long-E (happy, puppy, silly). Sort by that and let them find the pattern themselves. 🎉 The big moment is a promise kept: in Week 32 the class was told why would earn its letters one day — today it does. Take why down off the heart wall with real ceremony and let them SPELL it (wh-y) for the first time in thirteen weeks. Only 'on' (Week 11) and 'why' (today) ever cross from faith to readable — make it feel like the graduation-eve gift it is.
